findings.summary: The model claimed interleaved attempts could bypass the phase barrier.
The model claimed saturating byte accumulation could bypass the final bound.
The model requested heuristic detection of secrets in URL hosts or other components.
The model claimed usize-to-u64 fail-closed conversion could game resource accounting.
The model suggested more granular uppercase-versus-invalid-hex diagnostics.

findings.reason: The append validator rejects ordinal regression and rejects advancement while any attempt in the previous phase remains open. Dedicated phase-regression and all-open-attempt barrier tests pass.
Saturation produces u64::MAX, which is fail-closed against every finite configured ceiling. Pre-deserialization allocation is separately documented as an integration boundary.

Userinfo and passwords are rejected mechanically. Guessing whether arbitrary host, path, or query text is a secret would introduce the brittle semantic heuristic the architecture prohibits; typed broker integration is the declared next gate.
The supported 64-bit targets cannot represent a usize larger than u64. The fallback is u64::MAX and therefore fail-closed under finite bounds.
Canonical parsing is correct; splitting one diagnostic variant would improve ergonomics but not correctness or security.

limitations_retained: Artifact bytes are not fetched or attested by the typed crate.
Blind disclosure and the consuming seal are in-memory and not externally anchored.
Callers must enforce a transport frame limit before deserialization.
